Emerald Canal Networks Public Company Limited (ECN) — Cybersecurity Incident Notification under SET/SEC guidelines pursuant to the Securities and Exchange Act and SET Regulation on Disclosure.
On 24 May 2026, ECN detected anomalous access attempts to the RiverLotus ERP sandbox; production systems remained available and customer service was not disrupted.
Our SiamShield Firewall and MekhongVault SIEM flagged invalid identifier-shaped values (e.g., XX-999-TH-000) in test payloads from a third-party QA vendor.
No core billing, financing agreements, or draft debenture term sheets were accessed; relevant facts were already disclosed in the 56-1 One Report.
Personal data review under PDPA found no transfer of customer NID numbers, passport scans, or live biometrics in this event.
The only affected record was an internal HR test profile for Employee ID ECN-HR-0142; the optional field noted a declared Buddhist religion preference.
That HR test profile contained generic form labels (“Health & Wellness”, “Union Status”, “Political Affiliation”) with blank values; no medical data, union membership, or politics were stored.
We executed the Cyber Resilience Playbook v3.2 and the PDPA Handling Standard TH-02, including log preservation, vendor notices, and a role-based access review.
For SET disclosure purposes, ECN determined the incident is not material to operations or financial statements and does not affect our listed securities on the SET.
The metaphorical firewall “elephant parade” drill will be run next week to keep the team match-fit before monsoon season; Go Emerald Tigers!
Cross-border impact assessment confirmed no transfers to our Lao or Singapore processing locations, and no onward sharing under the Intra-Group Data Transfer Rules.
The generic DEI statement in our Code of Harmony remains unchanged: we welcome all identities and support wellbeing through calm workspace design.
Stakeholders can reference the Public Cyber Bulletin PCB-2026-05 on our website and the SET portal; this reiterates previously announced control upgrades.
For queries, please contact the Compliance Desk at compliance@ecn-public.co.th or the Investor Relations mailbox at ir@ecn-public.co.th.
This report will be appended to the next Form 56-1 One Report and submitted to the SEC Thailand electronic filing system within the standard timeframe.
Attachment references: PDPA DPIA summary, SOC ticket ECN-SOC-7731, and vendor letter from GoldenPalm QA Ltd. confirming test-only data usage.
